worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's measurement that Opus 4.7 tokenizes 1.46x more text and up to 3x more for images at the same per-token price is the sharpest cost-impact analysis in the feed — directly affects API budget decisions.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost spike is resolution-dependent, not universal — changes the practical takeaway from the prior post.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Visual map of Claude Code's 512k-line codebase — the agent loop and orchestration structure laid bare, useful for anyone building on top of it.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Continuation of Opus 4.7 quality backlash: confident-but-wrong behavior forcing fallback to 4.6 is a concrete agentic deployment risk signal.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's framing that OpenAI's decision to publish o1 publicly may have handed DeepSeek and competitors the key insight is a sharp strategic lens on open vs. closed AI positioning.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build + Grok Computer expected as a unified desktop app is a direct competitive move against Codex and Claude Code worth tracking.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: The most-engaged post in the feed captures the Claude community's split mood on Opus 4.7 — useful sentiment read at 3,934 likes.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest, an open-source model router claiming 70% cost reduction for agent workloads, is directly relevant to AI product cost strategy.

voices.why: Publishing the only empirical token-cost measurements for Opus 4.7 with actual tooling — the most grounded technical voice on model economics in this feed.
His o1 strategic framing adds genuine competitive-history context relevant to AI product positioning decisions.
The 30-day roadmap take and agent-native systems-from-first-principles thread are sharp product strategy takes with real engagement.

examples: @aakashgupta posting about tennis longevity, Nolan films, NASA capsules, and artillery shells — content farm scatter-shot at virality
@RoundtableSpace crypto/trading bot posts (AAVE exploit, Polymarket whale bot, 'we are aping in' engagement bait)
newsletter self-promo spam (@aakashgupta posting 'subscribe to my newsletter' 6+ times with near-zero engagement)
